PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Eugene Sober appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court questions Mr. Sober's understanding of probate and his ability to administer the estate without representation of Counsel. Mr. Sober stated that his Brother was never married and has no children, that the parents are deceased, that he has read the Nolo Book and that he has submitted the necessary papers. Mr. Sober also stated that he has some law experience in engineering matters, that he is a registered professional engineer in several States including California, that his Brother had no real estate and no vehicle, that he lived in a residential hotel and that his Brother had one brokerage account in which he is the beneficiary. In addition, Mr. Sober stated that there are some credit cards to take care of for small amounts, that he is not aware of anyone objecting, spoke of his level of education, stated that he is not aware of any controversy, that he is named in the will as the Executor and that he is the only beneficiary. The Court asked Mr. Sober if he is aware of what things he cannot do as an Executor. Mr. Sober stated that read about the things he is unable do as the Executor, that he does not think they apply to him because there is no real estate or jewelry, that he needs to create a bank account with an EIN number, that they can only be California accounts and that he will petition the Court to release the funds to him. Mr. Sober also stated that he understands that the bank accounts cannot exceed $250,000.00, that the only known liability is that the Manager wanted money to fix up the apartment, that there are some taxes to pay, that his Brother had no job and lived off of retirement income, and that the credit cards are less than $300.00 for all three accounts. In addition, Mr. Sober stated that the credit card companies will not release any information to him without a Court order, that the statements received in the mail indicate small amounts of liabilities, that he paid $14,000.00 of his own money to transport his Brother back and for the funeral expenses and that no one has come forth. Furthermore, Mr. Sober stated that the $46,000.00 estimate of liabilities includes the $14,000.00 and $27,800.00 for round trip airfare to California and his stay in a hotel while cleaning the apartment, that the estimated tax amount is for personal taxes, not estate taxes, that he is bondable and that he has spoken with some bond companies. The Court explained that the purpose of requiring a bond is to protect the creditors. The Court stated that it will set bond at $20,000.00 and appoint Mr. Sober as the Executor of the will dated November 7, 2006 with limited authority. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for January 12, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is set for September 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. Sober that a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is due one week before the hearing date. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-12-2020 Continued To Sep-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
